name: Chocolate-Coconut Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease two 9-inch round cake pans; dust the bottoms and sides of the pans with cocoa.
2. In a heavy saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate, stirring often until smooth. Remove from heat.
3. In a large bowl, measure the remaining ingredients and add the melted chocolate. Beat with a mixer on low speed until well mixed, scraping the bowl frequently. Increase speed to high and beat for 2 minutes. Stir in coconut.
4. Spread batter evenly into pans. Bake for 25-30 minutes.
5. Cool cakes in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes; then invert onto racks to cool completely.
6. When cakes are cool, prepare the filling. In a small bowl, with a mixer on medium speed, beat cream until stiff peaks form.
7. Cut each cake horizontally into two layers and place one layer on a serving plate. Spread half the filling over it.
8. Top with a second layer; spread half the cream over it. Place another cake layer on top; spread remaining filling over it.
9. Top with the last layer and spread the remaining cream over it.
Filling:
In a saucepan over medium heat, heat evaporated milk, packed brown sugar, butter, unsweetened chocolate, and slightly beaten egg yolks until the chocolate melts and the mixture coats a spoon (about 10 minutes), stirring often. Do not bring to a boil or it will curdle. Remove from heat; stir in chopped walnuts and vanilla. Cool until thick enough to spread, stirring occasionally.
